---
title: "Integrated Reference Architecture"
chapter_id: "integrated-reference-architecture"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "viea"
  - "scf"
  - "vcm_public"
  - "planforge"
  - "talos"
  - "spinoza"
  - "octopus_router"
  - "rmi"
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "alignment_field"
  - "cgs"
  - "cognitive_loop_closure"
  - "moecot"
  - "vcm_editable"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Integrated Reference Architecture

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `integrated-reference-architecture` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `viea`, `scf`, `vcm_public`, `planforge`, `talos`, `spinoza`, `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `benchmaxxing`, `alignment_field`, `cgs`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot`, `vcm_editable` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

Readers need to see how the layers operate as one machine from intent to governed action and improvement.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Layer chapters can still feel disconnected unless the book traces the complete control flow and artifacts.

## Core Claim

[integrated-reference-architecture.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] The stack can be specified as an integrated reference architecture with typed handoffs, authority stops, artifacts, evidence updates, and self-improvement gates.

## Mechanism

- Trace user intent through constitution, governance, planning, VCM, routing, verification, execution, evidence, and improvement.
- Show the compression loop embedded inside the broader governance, memory, routing, and verification stack.
- Show which artifact each layer emits.
- Identify where authority can stop or reroute the process.

## Interfaces

- All stack layers participate.
- Artifacts and evidence ledgers provide continuity.
- SCF gates control improvement.

## Invariants

- No layer bypasses governance.
- Artifacts remain traceable.
- Self-improvement follows evidence and authority gates.

## Failure modes

- Untraceable handoffs.
- Planning/execution collapse.
- Self-improvement without evaluator integrity.

## Minimal implementation

A reference flow diagram, interface table, and end-to-end trace example marked conceptual until implemented.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| End-to-end intent trace test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Artifact continuity audit | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Authority stop-condition test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `scf` | Stable Capability Fields | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Use public release v1.0 when available. Stable boundaries, replacement, bounded authority, recoverable evolution. |
| `vcm_public` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1 | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Public VCM release. Governed protocol for compiled working context. |
| `planforge` | PlanForge | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Planning substrate. Goal-to-execution compilation, hierarchical decomposition, DAG planning, scheduling, intelligence arbitrage. |
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. |
| `octopus_router` | Octopus Router Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Lightweight head/router with dynamically loaded specialist arms and local boundaries. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `benchmaxxing` | Benchmaxxing: The Performance Ratchet | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmarks as pressure surfaces, saturation -> regression, harder frontier, anti-Goodhart safeguards. |
| `alignment_field` | Field of God / Alignment Field family | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Primary Alignment Field draft found. Use with Ethica Mechanica and Eternal Code. |
| `cgs` | Compact Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Smallest adequate structure that can generate/govern target without hiding residual complexity. |
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Cognitive Loop Closure | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Repeated cognition should become procedural memory / verified tools. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |
| `vcm_editable` | Virtual_Context_Memory_v1.0_Editable | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Editable version with evidence-carrying planner-guided context compiler framing. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
